title: 1.1: Tests that when a user is linked to a partner and alias options hide built-in domains but show partner domains, the list of available alias suffixes places the user's chosen default domain first when that default is a non-partner domain
1.2: Tests that when the user's default alias domain is hidden, the list of available alias suffixes excludes hidden domains and does not rank the hidden default first

1.3: Tests that when a free user has a premium-only domain as default, the list of available alias suffixes includes only domains the user is allowed to use and does not rank the premium default first

1.4: Tests that when a user has custom and built-in domains with options enabling both, the returned alias suffixes follow the expected format (optional random prefix, @, domain) and at least one suffix includes a random prefix
1.5: Tests that when retrieving the list for a user with a default domain set, the default domain appears in the list and each domain appears exactly once with no duplicates
2.1: Places the test for default domain with restricted alias options in the alias suffix test file
2.2: Places the test for hidden and premium default domain behavior in the alias suffix test file
2.3: Places the test for suffix format and default-once behavior in the alias suffix test file
3.1: All added tests use the pytest framework
3.2: All added tests follow the test_ prefix naming pattern
3.3: All added tests use assert statements to verify behavior
4.1: All added tests use Session and model fixtures or helpers consistent with the existing alias suffix tests
